scope.summary: D4 privilege-matrix fixture wired into the rig: the `oracle_l1.sh privilege-matrix` command provisions the no-flashback + catalog-blind principals and emits the env the live test consumes; the two #[ignore]d test cases reproduce the recorded failing half through the wired command.
out_of_scope: The green half: A3a preflights missing DBMS_FLASHBACK EXECUTE before session-state cleanup, A3b keeps quarantine only for genuine teardown failures, and A1a requires positive catalog visibility before a read-only proof. Those fix beads own flipping the two tests green (delete their #[ignore]).
Wiring the lane into CI or the one-command `run` cycle — it is a standalone rig command, at parity with drcp-identity.

entry_points.name: D4 rig command provisions the fixture and emits env
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh privilege-matrix --log` exited 0 on free23: loaded oracle_l1_privilege_matrix.sql as SYSDBA inside FREEPDB1, the fixture's catalog proof found exactly one enabled SELECT VPD policy and one virtual column on ORACLEMCP_D4_OWNER.ORACLEMCP_D4_GUARDED, and the command emitted ORACLEMCP_D4_DSN (//localhost:1522/FREEPDB1), both restricted principals, and ORACLEMCP_D4_CATALOG_OBJECT_ID (the rig-verified object id) on stdout with log events on stderr.

entry_points.name: D4 no-flashback failing half (expected-red)
entry_points.trace: With the emitted env exported, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test privilege_matrix_live -- --ignored --nocapture` ran d4_no_flashback_principal_gets_typed_refusal_and_connection_stays_usable: the initial request was typed FLASHBACK_CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE, then DBMS_FLASHBACK.DISABLE cleanup returned ORA-06550/PLS-00201 (identifier 'DBMS_FLASHBACK' must be declared) and the thin connection was quarantined as unknown_discarded, so the post-refusal usability assertion failed. This is the recorded failing half A3a/A3b flip green.

entry_points.name: D4 catalog-blind failing half (expected-red)
entry_points.trace: Same run, d4_catalog_blind_principal_cannot_prove_the_known_guarded_table_read_only: resolved_relations_read_purity returned ProvenReadOnly where the assertion requires Unknown — the catalog-blind principal's successful-empty ALL_POLICIES/ALL_TAB_COLS probes fall through to a read-only proof despite the known VPD policy + virtual column on the target. This is the fail-open A1a closes.

entry_points.name: D4 script hygiene
entry_points.trace: `bash -n scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh` and `shellcheck -S warning scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh` both exited 0 after the wiring.

limitations: The lane ran against the already-running local Free23 rig container and is recorded as scoped integration evidence, not as a committed live-evidence artifact.
The two test cases remain #[ignore]d; this close records the wired lane and the failing half per the closure convention. The green half is owned by A3a/A3b and A1a.
The lane is a standalone rig command, not yet part of the one-command `run` cycle or CI.

known_defects.description: A principal without EXECUTE ON DBMS_FLASHBACK is permanently quarantined (unknown_discarded) because read_query_as_of runs DBMS_FLASHBACK.DISABLE cleanup before any capability preflight, treating a predictable missing-privilege error like an unprovable teardown. A3a/A3b own the fix.

The VPD-policy and virtual-column catalog gates treat a successful-empty probe as harmless, so a catalog-blind principal's dictionary responses fall through to ProvenReadOnly despite known hidden behavior on the target — a fail-open. A1a owns requiring positive catalog visibility before a read-only proof.
